it:26.2: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
it:26.3:
"If beings knew, as I know, the results of giving and sharing, they would not eat without having given, nor would the stain of selfishness overcome their minds. Even if it were their last bite, their last mouthful, they would not eat without having shared, if there were someone to receive their gift. But because beings do not know, as I know, the results of giving and sharing, they eat without having given. The stain of selfishness overcomes their minds."
If beings knew what the Great Seer said, how the result of sharing has such great fruit, then …
it:26.4: Subduing the stain of selfishness with brightened awareness, they'd give in season to the noble ones, where a gift bears great fruit.
it:26.5:
Having given food as an offering to those worthy of offerings, many donors, when they pass away from here, the human state, go to heaven.
They, having gone there to heaven, rejoice, enjoying sensual pleasures. Unselfish, they partake of the result of sharing.
it:26.6: This, too, was the meaning of what was said by the Blessed One, so I have heard.
